rationale: The citing paper examines how metric framing (productivity vs time metrics) distorts time-savings judgments, while the cited paper studies how time-interval framing (extent vs date) changes temporal discounting, so there is plausible conceptual overlap on time-description effects. However, the matched evidence is only a DOI-level reference record with no in-text citation context, making it impossible to determine whether this is a substantive theoretical source or just background mention.
